status: DRAFT, genesis run from a single SOP. Routing not yet meaningful with one project.

projects.name: entity-reference-verification
projects.task_type: Decide whether a yellow-boxed target region and a green-boxed reference show the same real-world entity, pick one of six verdicts, and write a feature-named reason.
projects.stimulus_source: real_asset (identity hinges on fine perceptual detail, generated images are not trustworthy ground truth)

skills.name: Instruction Reading
skills.description: Read the entity type and label first and load the identity criteria that apply to that type before judging the images.

skills.name: Attention to Detail
skills.description: Notice the small construction or marking detail, a strap width, a logo position, a jersey number, a chest marking, that decides identity.

skills.name: Calibrated Judgment
skills.description: Choose definitely only when the deciding feature is clearly visible, drop to likely under doubt, and skip a genuine coin flip rather than guess.

skills.name: Objectivity
skills.description: Judge only on identity signals for the entity type and ignore non-identity content such as outfit, background, lighting, weather, and breed.

skills.name: Identity Criteria Knowledge
skills.description: Know what establishes identity for each entity type, face for a person, construction for a garment, individual markings for an animal, structure for a location.

skills.name: Comparison
skills.description: Weigh the target against the reference feature by feature and decide whether the identity features match or conflict.

skills.name: Difference Detection
skills.description: Spot the specific feature that conflicts between target and reference, such as a moved logo or a number present in one image and absent in the other.

skills.name: Visual Quality Assessment
skills.description: Judge whether a deciding feature is clearly visible or too occluded, distant, folded, or low resolution to support a confident call.

skills.name: Data Integrity Screening
skills.description: Recognize a broken input that must be flagged rather than verified, such as the same image on both sides, a watermark, a wrong box, or a same-photoshoot pair.

skills.name: Clear Writing
skills.description: Write a short reason that names the specific identity features used, so a reviewer can audit the decision from the features alone.
